name: NAFLD - Elevated ALT/AST Lab Query
description: Tests ability to find NAFLD patients via elevated liver enzymes. ALT (alanine aminotransferase) and AST (aspartate aminotransferase) are the standard liver function tests. ALT >40 IU/L is the traditional cutoff for liver injury (modern guidelines suggest >25-30 for women and >35 for men). NAFLD typically presents with ALT > AST (ratio inverted from alcoholic liver disease where AST > ALT). Synthea generates ALT 60-200 IU/L for active NAFLD patients.
prompt: Find patients with elevated liver enzyme lab results including alanine aminotransferase (ALT) or aspartate aminotransferase (AST) greater than 40 IU/L.

prompts.naive: Find patients with high liver enzymes.
prompts.broad: Find patients with elevated liver function tests — alanine aminotransferase (ALT) or aspartate aminotransferase (AST) values greater than 40 IU/L, the traditional clinical threshold for liver injury workup.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources with elevated liver enzymes. Use LOINC codes 1742-6 (ALT) and 1920-8 (AST). Filter by value-quantity greater than 40 IU/L. Two queries: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1742-6&value-quantity=gt40||U/L (ALT) and Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1920-8&value-quantity=gt40||U/L (AST). Or combined as comma-separated codes with value filter.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated ALT or AST. Common in NAFLD; cross-indication with hepatitis, drug-induced injury, etc.

required_codes.display: Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Aspartate aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
